name: Toshiko Akiyoshi
description: Japanese American jazz pianist, composer, arranger, band master
abstract: Toshiko Akiyoshi (秋吉敏子 or 穐吉敏子, Akiyoshi Toshiko; born 12 December 1929) is a Japanese jazz pianist, composer, arranger, and bandleader. Akiyoshi received fourteen Grammy Award nominations and was the first woman to win Best Arranger and Composer awards in Down Beat magazine's annual Readers' Poll.

quotes.text: But twice we had real players, in Japan. That was a luxury. And also here at the concert … uh, uh … the one time was at Carnegie Hall [recorded and released as Carnegie Hall Concert by Columbia in 1991], and then another time at Sankei Hall in Japan, they had a real player. But most of the time, for example, we used to play every Monday night at Birdland in New York, and every Monday night, and I had a tape recorder set for that and prerecorded. So, yeah, that‟s … you know, if you are a classical organization, you can afford to have, their budget will provide it, even though it‟s like a twenty-minute piece of music and all you need is one note [chuckles], they will, that musician was hired. We don‟t have that kind of luxury, so….
quotes.caption: Mentions Carnegie Hall concert recorded in 1991
quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: Yes I did. I used to be one of the regular subs that played with her band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know how you rate Toshiko Akiyoshi, but as far as I’m concerned she’s an excellent writer and she gets a lot of great sounds, chords and textures out of that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Schenectady, New York, March 28, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I’ll tell you who they had. Toshiko [Akiyoshi] had just come over [from Japan], and she did a stint in there, or maybe more then one, when I was gone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...there’s only one other big band that was happening at that particular time, Toshiko Akiyoshi had her big band. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Probably the most recent group I heard, well, this past year, six months ago, was the Toshiko Akiyoshi - Lew Tabackin band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...I did an interview with Toshiko Akiyoshi. And she had related... [INTERVIEWEE] In Manchuria I believe.
